summary:
The correct answer is C because Th1 cell-derived interferon-gamma drives critical anti-mycobacterial responses including enhanced phagolysosomal fusion and inducible nitric oxide synthase activity leading to effective bacterial destruction inside infected macrophages. This pathway represents one of the most clinically significant immune mechanisms against *Mycobacterium tuberculosis*.

The summary should be concise yet fully capture all essential elements from Agent2’s response regarding immunological function during active TB disease progression.

Final Answer:

\boxed{\text{C}}  
Interferon-gamma (IFN-γ) from Th1 cells stimulates phagosome-lysosome fusion and induces nitric oxide production in macrophages—key defenses against intracellular *Mycobacterium tuberculosis*. No other cytokine listed plays this central role in macrophage-mediated antibacterial immunity.
